title: The RPCS3 emulator now runs 75% of PS3 games on PC, nearing the point of no longer needing Sony's firmware
summary: The open-source emulator RPCS3 has made three-quarters of the PlayStation 3 catalog playable on computers, a milestone that coincides with the announcement of the closure of the PS3 and PS Vita digital store.

title: Google renames NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook and integrates it with its search application
summary: Google's AI-powered note-taking tool is being renamed Gemini Notebook, also allowing integration with third-party applications.

title: OpenAI presents Codex Micro, its first physical device with a joystick to control AI agents
summary: The device, an RGB macropod with 13 keys and a joystick, allows developers to interact with AI coding agents without having to type commands.

title: Linus Torvalds defends the use of AI in the Linux kernel and criticizes opponents
summary: The creator of Linux states that artificial intelligence is a useful tool and that those who oppose it can fork the project.

title: Growing discontent over having to manually opt out of artificial intelligence on each service
summary: Users and activists denounce the constant burden of having to opt out of AI systems, calling for changes in regulation.

title: China approves the launch of Apple Intelligence with Alibaba and Baidu models
summary: Chinese authorities have given the green light to Apple's artificial intelligence platform, which will use Alibaba and Baidu services to comply with local regulations.

title: AMD Ryzen 7 7700X3D arrives as a Newegg exclusive and early reviews compare it to the 7800X3D
summary: AMD's new 3D V-Cache processor launches in North America exclusively through Newegg for $329, and reviews show performance lower than the 7800X3D but at a similar price.

title: Lenovo presents the first laptop with an inkjet-printed OLED display from TCL CSOT
summary: The Legion R9000P features a 240 Hz OLED panel manufactured using inkjet printing technology, a world first for laptops.

title: Spotify expands free managed accounts for children to new markets
summary: The streaming platform now allows free users to create supervised accounts for children under 13, expanding the feature to more regions.

title: OpenAI uses AI to attack its own AI and strengthen GPT-5.6 security
summary: The GPT-Red tool automates prompt injection tests to identify vulnerabilities in language models, surpassing human teams in effectiveness.

title: Moonshot launches Kimi 3, an AI model aiming to compete with Anthropic Opus 4.8
summary: Chinese startup Moonshot presents Kimi K3, its new language model that promises to approach the performance of industry leaders.

title: Two Scattered Spider hackers sentenced to 5.5 years in prison for £29 million cyberattack on TfL
summary: British police arrested two young people who dismantled the operations of the infamous hacker group, and they were sentenced for the attack on London's public transport.

title: Sony faces criticism for deleting digitally purchased movies and defending equal prices for digital games
summary: Former PlayStation executives justify that digital games will not be cheaper without discs, while Sony deletes titles purchased by users, generating controversy over digital ownership.

title: Meta implements parental alerts for suicide risks in its AI chatbot, while a study warns it could be a propaganda machine
summary: The social network notifies guardians if their teenagers talk about self-harm with the AI, while the Oversight Board points out that chatbots could spread propaganda on a massive scale.

title: Germany subjects Google and Perplexity AI summaries to media law, while Anthropic urges faster regulation
summary: In a pioneering decision, Germany classifies AI assistants as media services, while Anthropic pushes states to accelerate AI regulation.

title: OnePlus halts its operations in the United States and Europe
summary: The smartphone brand suspends its sales and activities in Western markets, according to industry reports.

title: Elon Musk spent nearly $1 billion on an energy company to power xAI
summary: According to regulatory documents, Musk acquired APR Energy, which owns gas and diesel turbines with a capacity of over 1 gigawatt, to supply electricity to his artificial intelligence centers.

title: Nvidia and Japan unveil the world's first national AI infrastructure
summary: The Noetra consortium will build a 140 MW Rubin AI factory with 27,500 GPUs, marking a milestone in public-private collaboration for artificial intelligence.

title: Zoom fixes critical vulnerability in Windows that could allow account theft
summary: The security flaw in the videoconferencing application for Windows exposed users to possible account hijacking, already patched by the company.

title: Hundreds protest outside Bethesda headquarters against Xbox layoffs
summary: Protesters demand that Microsoft reconsider the staff cuts in its video game studios, which have affected numerous employees.
